e2eFlows: List/detail split: nav-automations → list screen (no canvas, no design-email) → New automation → /automations/new builder → save → back to list; open a row → /automations/:id builder.
Lifecycle via ActionMenu: publish (draft→active), pause (active→paused), resume (paused→active), archive (→archived, row leaves default list); each spins+locks; archive confirmed via app-dialog.
Capability visibility: marketer sees lifecycle actions; accounting does not (role-visibility pattern).
Full-node journey build smoke: assemble trigger→wait→hour-window→if→update-profile→send→webhook→exit, configure each via its drawer, attach a sendable template, publish to active.
Enrollment counts: a row shows {active/completed/exited/failed} reflecting automation_enrollments after an enroll.
implUnits: 1. (TEST-FIRST) Write the failing tests above. Start with services/local-api/test/automation-lifecycle.integration.test.ts + automation-enrollment-counts.integration.test.ts + the journey integration test; then the e2e specs; then paused-automation-not-advanced.

2. Backend lifecycle endpoints (services/local-api): add handlers pauseAutomation/resumeAutomation/archiveAutomation (scopedQuery UPDATE automations SET status WHERE id; 404 on cross-ws; typed 409 for an illegal transition via nextLifecycle). Register routes POST /automations/:id/{pause,resume,archive} = manage_content in routes.ts and HANDLERS map in handlers.ts. No migration — automations.status already documents draft|active|paused|archived.

3. GET /automations enrollment counts: extend listAutomations to LEFT JOIN a workspace-scoped GROUP BY (automation_id,status) aggregate (or a lateral) and shape via automationCountsShape → return {id,name,status,counts:{active,completed,exited,failed}}.

4. Runner pause gate (services/automation-runner/src/run.ts): in the locked read, also SELECT the automation status; only advance when status='active' (paused/archived → no-op result). Preserve FOR UPDATE/legacy-CAS, MAX_STEPS_PER_TICK, dedupe_key — no change to outbox/webhook semantics.

5. Web: SPLIT AutomationBuilder.tsx into AutomationsList.tsx (the LIST: PageHeader with ONLY 'New automation' — DELETE the design-email Button — a table/list of rows with status Badge + enrollment-counts cell + an ActionMenu of Open/Pause/Resume/Archive mirroring BroadcastComposer) and AutomationDetail.tsx (the existing canvas builder body keyed by id, with a automations-back button). Keep the canvas/palette/node-editor/publish code in the detail component verbatim (move, don't rewrite).

6. Web routing (AppShell.tsx + router): #/automations → <AutomationsList/>; #/automations/new and #/automations/:id → <AutomationDetail id?/> (mirror the broadcasts rest === 'new' branch). Preserve the editorReturn flow: returning from a send-node Design email lands on /automations/:id detail (takeReturnedTo) — now it opens the detail page directly instead of the combined screen. navigate('/automations/'+id) on row open / after create.
7. Web list actions: wire pause/resume/archive to the new endpoints (api.post, button RETURNS the promise → kit auto-lock), askConfirm for archive, showToast on success/error, reload list after.
8. toneFor: add 'paused' (warn) and 'archived' (neutral/danger) keyword handling in web/src/ui/kit.tsx so the badges color correctly (tiny).

9. Seed (web/e2e/seed.ts): ensure CAMP_A (Welcome journey) stays for the list/detail open test; add a seeded enrollment or two (varied status) so the counts cell is non-zero in e2e; ensure an allowlisted webhook host + a sendable template copy exist for the journey smoke. Keep cdp_e2e re-seed contract.

10. Docs + hygiene: update CLAUDE.md (automations = list + /automations/:id detail; design-email only in send-node editor; lifecycle pause/resume/archive endpoints + capability; enrollment counts on the list; runner honors automation status); bump root package.json (minor — feature). Apply NO migration (status already supports the values); if a future archived-filter index is wanted, it's optional and would go to cdp + cdp_e2e via a node pg script.
11. Rebuild changed packages (tsc -b) the local-api imports from dist; restart dev API; run pnpm typecheck + full Vitest + e2e cold.
mocks: SES: aws-sdk-client-mock (mockClient(SESv2/SES SendEmailCommand)) in the dispatcher leg of the journey test — assert SendEmail called with the workspace Configuration Set, only AFTER suppression/cap/quiet-hours/verified-domain gate; NEVER a real send.

Webhook HTTP: an INJECTED WebhookHttpClient (from @cdp/runner-webhook) fake that captures {url, method, headers, body} and can be configured to throw/timeout/return non-2xx — never hits a real host; reuse webhook-node-tick.integration.test's pattern.
SQS: a CapturingSqs stub (push MessageBody) for the runner's dispatch enqueue (no LocalStack needed for the unit/integration tiers here).
Clock: deps.now() injected as a fixed Date per tick (NOT a mock library) — the runner's documented injection point for wait/hour-window determinism.
secret-crypto: reuse the real @cdp/db decryptSecret/isEncryptedSecret with a test KEK (CDP_MASTER_KEY) so the webhook auth-header decryption path is exercised, not stubbed.
Real (NOT mocked): Postgres (adminPool / local-api createApp over the real pool) and the real browser (Playwright) for UI tiers.
result.notes: WHERE 'Design email' is today: AutomationBuilder.tsx PageHeader actions (data-testid="design-email", lines ~266-275) — a standalone Button navigating to /editor. Phase-7 REMOVES this from the LIST page; the SAME design-email affordance must remain reachable INSIDE the send-node editor (NodeEditor's send body already has a Design email path for the automation send instance — that stays). LIST/DETAIL SPLIT: AutomationBuilder.tsx is currently ONE combined screen — a name/canvas builder on top + a automation list below — rendered at BOTH #/automations and #/automations/* (AppShell screenFor returns <AutomationBuilder/> for both). Split it: AutomationsList (list only) at #/automations, AutomationDetail (the canvas builder, the bulk of today's file moved verbatim) at #/automations/new and #/automations/:id, mirroring BroadcastComposer/BroadcastWizard + the AppShell broadcasts branch. The editorReturn flow already targets /automations/:id (takeReturnedTo) so returning from a send-node Design email will now naturally land on the detail page. NO MIGRATION NEEDED: automations.status (0005_messaging.sql line 26) already documents draft|active|paused|archived and is a free-text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'draft' column; automation_enrollments.status already covers active|completed|exited|failed — pause/resume/archive are plain UPDATEs, enrollment counts a GROUP BY. (If you want to harden, an optional CHECK constraint could be added to cdp+cdp_e2e via a node pg script, but the project convention favors no migration when the column already supports the values — recommend none.) FULL-JOURNEY DRIVER: the runner is advanced with deps.now() returning a FIXED Date per tick (the documented injection point in RunDeps; full-lifecycle.integration.test already does t0/t2). The journey test composes the existing real-Postgres patterns into ONE end-to-end: enroll via the LIVE local-api POST /profiles/:id/events (closes the phase-3 gap — handlers.ts sendProfileEvent already calls enrollFromEvent on the same tx, so this is exercising the wired path, not new code) PLUS manual+segment enroll, then tick through wait→hour-window→if→set_attribute→send→webhook→exit, asserting: an event-sourced attribute write, an outbox→dispatcher messages_log row with automation_id (SES mocked, gated), the injected webhook fired once (SSRF/allowlist enforced, failures isolated), idempotent re-tick (dedupe_key + per-node webhook marker), all workspace-scoped (runStatementsInWorkspaceTx guard + a parallel WS_B left untouched). The ONLY backend behavior that may be genuinely missing is the runner reading automation.status to halt a PAUSED automation — the paused-automation-not-advanced test drives adding that gate inside the locked tick (preserving FOR UPDATE/CAS/idempotency); everything else (webhook, set-attribute, send, hour-window, all three enroll kinds) already has green integration coverage, so phase-7's real new surface is the UI list/detail split + lifecycle endpoints/counts + the combined acceptance test.
pureFunctions: automationCountsShape(rows): fold the GROUP BY (automation_id,status) rows into a per-automation {active,completed,exited,failed} record defaulting missing buckets to 0 (testable without DB; used to shape GET /automations).

nextLifecycle(current, action): map (status, 'pause'|'resume'|'archive') → next status or a typed rejection reason (e.g. resume only from 'paused'); pure transition table reused by the handler and a unit test.
isEnrollableAutomationStatus(status): boolean — only 'active' enrolls/advances (reused by the pause gate + enroll cores assertion).

REUSE existing pures: computeWaitNextRunAt, parseIso8601DurationSeconds, isWaitElapsed, processNode, evaluateBranch, buildBranchMatchQuery, parseEventEnrollmentTrigger, evaluateEventPayloadFilter, buildEnrollmentInsert, decideReenrollment, zonedInputToUtcIso/tzOffsetMs (hour-window + wait tz math) — no new tz code.
reuse: BroadcastComposer.tsx: the list+detail routing model, ActionMenu row actions, agoLabel/whenLabel, the no-domain banner pattern, status Badge+toneFor, spinner+lock send button wiring — MIRROR for AutomationsList.
AppShell.tsx broadcasts branch (rest === 'new' ? <Wizard/> : <Wizard id/>) — copy for automations list vs detail.

automation-runner: runEnrollment + RunDeps (injected now/sqs/webhookClient/withTx), buildSweepQuery, processNode, computeWaitNextRunAt, hour-window logic, set_attribute event-sourcing, the per-(automation,profile,node) dedupe_key — the journey test composes these EXACTLY as full-lifecycle.integration.test + webhook-node-tick + set-attribute-event-sourced + hour-window.integration already do.
automation-send-dispatcher-instance.integration.test.ts: the runner-outbox → dispatcher-core → messages_log(automation_id) chaining (SES mocked) — the journey test's send leg copies this.

enroll-from-event.integration.test.ts: the enrollFromEvent assertions; the journey test EXTENDS it by driving the LIVE local-api POST /profiles/:id/events (createApp over the real pool) instead of calling the core directly.

local-api handlers: scopedQuery, ownsResource, validateDefinitionOr400, enrollDepsOnClient/enrollDepsOnPool, the existing activateAutomation gate (publish) — the new pause/resume/archive sit beside activate and reuse scopedQuery + the 404 pattern.
ui kit: ActionMenu, Badge, Button (promise auto-lock), PageHeader, EmptyState, Card; ui/dialog askConfirm; ui/toast showToast — all already used by BroadcastComposer.
web/e2e/helpers.ts (loginAs, DEV_MKT/DEV_ACCT) + seed.ts (CAMP_A, DEV_* logins, sendable template) — reuse for the new e2e specs.
@cdp/shared timeZoneList/zonedInputToUtcIso/tzOffsetMs — the ONE tz implementation for hour-window + wait math (no new tz code).

assertions: loginAs(DEV_MKT) → nav-automations → the LIST screen renders (new testid automations-list-screen / list testid automation-list), NOT the canvas: page.getByTestId('automation-canvas') has count 0 and page.getByTestId('automation-builder') is NOT present on the list route (#/automations).
The list page has NO top-level Design email button: await expect(page.getByTestId('design-email')).toHaveCount(0) while on #/automations (regression for the removed PageHeader affordance).

The list has a 'New automation' action (automation-new); clicking it navigates to a SEPARATE detail route (assert page.url() matches /#\/automations\/(new|[0-9a-f-]{36})$/ and automation-canvas becomes visible) — the builder is NOT the top-level page.

Opening the seeded 'Welcome journey' row (automation-open / row click) navigates to #/automations/<CAMP_A> and the canvas reconstructs the DSL (node-trigger/node-wait/node-condition visible) — list→detail routing mirrors broadcasts.

From inside the detail builder, a send node's editor still exposes Design email (open node-send editor drawer → design-email present) — proving email design lives ONLY inside a send-node editor, not on the list.
Each list row shows the lifecycle status badge (automation-status) and an enrollment-counts summary cell (new testid automation-counts showing active/completed/exited/failed).
Back navigation from the detail builder returns to the list (a automations-back button mirroring broadcasts-back) → #/automations.
testFiles.criterion: The automations screen is a LIST (like broadcasts); creating/opening a automation routes to a SEPARATE /automations/:id builder page; there is NO "Design email" button on the automations list page (email design is only inside a send-node editor). Verified in a REAL browser.

assertions: Build a minimal valid trigger→send→exit automation in the detail page, attach a sendable seeded template copy to the send node (reuse the publish-gate spec's attach flow), Publish → status badge flips to 'active' on the list (automation-status text 'active').

An ActionMenu (automation-actions kebab, mirroring broadcast-actions) on each list row exposes: Open/Edit, Pause (when active), Resume (when paused), Archive — each a server-calling button that spins+locks (no native confirm; Archive uses askConfirm → app-dialog).

Pause an active automation → POST /automations/:id/pause → status badge becomes 'paused'; Resume → POST /automations/:id/resume → back to 'active'; Archive → POST /automations/:id/archive → status 'archived' and the row drops from the default list (or shows archived filter).

Capability gating: log in as DEV_ACCT (accounting, lacks manage_content) — the Pause/Resume/Publish/Archive actions are absent/disabled and a direct POST returns 403 (assert via role-visibility pattern); the marketer (manage_content) sees and can use them.
Workspace-scoping: the WS_A automation list never shows WS_B automations; switching workspace re-scopes the list (AppShell key remounts).

Enrollment counts render live: after enrolling a profile (via manual enroll in the builder or seeded enrollment) the row's automation-counts active count reflects automation_enrollments grouped by status for that automation only (workspace-scoped).
testFiles.criterion: The automation list shows lifecycle status + enrollment counts; publish/pause/resume work and are capability-gated + workspace-scoped; an active automation enrolls + advances profiles. Verified in a REAL browser.

assertions: POST /automations/:id/pause on an 'active' automation sets status='paused' (UPDATE ... SET status='paused' WHERE id AND workspace_id); returns the new status; idempotent (pausing a paused automation is a no-op 200, not an error).

POST /automations/:id/resume on a 'paused' automation sets status='active'; resume on a non-paused (draft/archived) automation is rejected 409 with a typed reason (can only resume a paused automation).

POST /automations/:id/archive sets status='archived' from any state; an archived automation is excluded from enrollment (enrollFromEvent/enrollFromSegmentChange only target status='active') — regression-assert no new enrollment after archive.

Pause does NOT touch in-flight automation_enrollments rows directly; assert the runner skips a paused automation's enrollments (the sweep/runEnrollment re-checks automation status='active' inside the lock; a paused automation's due enrollment is left parked, not advanced) — drives this with the automation-runner core in the journey test, asserted here at the handler level that status flips only.

Tenant isolation (inv.1/inv.2): a pause/resume/archive POST with a :id belonging to WS_B under a WS_A token returns 404 (scopedQuery WHERE workspace_id = ctx.workspaceId AND id = $1, rowCount 0 → 404); workspace_id is never taken from the body.
Capability: routes.ts maps POST /automations/:id/{pause,resume,archive} to manage_content; a marketer passes, an accounting role 403s (dispatch enforce).
testFiles.criterion: publish/pause/resume/archive work and are capability-gated + workspace-scoped (REAL Postgres).

assertions: GET /automations returns each automation with status AND a counts object {active, completed, exited, failed} aggregated from automation_enrollments grouped by status, scoped to ctx.workspaceId and automation_id (LEFT JOIN / lateral count so a automation with no enrollments returns all-zero counts).

Counts are per-automation and workspace-scoped: seed two automations in WS_A with differing enrollment status mixes + one automation in WS_B; assert WS_A list shows the correct per-automation split and never sums WS_B rows.

A single workspace-scoped aggregate query (one round-trip, GROUP BY automation_id, status) — assert correctness for active/completed/exited/failed buckets and that an unknown status (defensive) does not break the shape.
GET /automations/:id continues to return the full definition + timezone unchanged (regression).
testFiles.criterion: The automation list shows lifecycle status + enrollment counts (REAL Postgres).

assertions: Setup against REAL Postgres (adminPool): one workspace (settings.timezone set, e.g. 'America/New_York'), a profile with email, a verified sending_domain + domain_senders row, an email_templates copy (kind='copy') with subject+sender_id+to_address set (sendable), and a automation whose definition is trigger(event)→wait→hour_of_day_window→condition→action(set_attribute)→action(send)→action(webhook)→exit, status='active', with the webhook url on a per-workspace allowlist host.

LIVE EVENT ENROLLMENT (closes the phase-3 gap): drive enrollment through the local-api createApp() by POSTing to POST /profiles/:id/events (the live route, marketer token) with the trigger eventType+payload → assert exactly ONE automation_enrollments row at current_node=trigger, status='active', workspace-scoped; replaying the same POST (different event_id) does NOT duplicate (ON CONFLICT once).

ALSO assert the other two trigger kinds enroll via their cores in the same workspace: enrollProfileManually (POST /automations/:id/enroll body {profile_id}) and enrollFromSegmentChange — each idempotent, each one row.

ADVANCE with an injected clock: build RunDeps with now()=fixed Date (reuse withWorkspaceTx/runStatementsInWorkspaceTx + an injected webhookClient capturing requests + a CapturingSqs). Tick at T0 → parks at wait; tick at T0+delay but OUTSIDE the hour window → parks/defers at hour_of_day_window (next_run_at moved into the window, DST-correct in the workspace tz); tick at a clock INSIDE the window → condition evaluates, set_attribute writes, send enqueues to outbox, webhook fires, enrollment completes.

UPDATE-PROFILE is event-sourced: assert set_attribute wrote BOTH the profiles.attributes change AND an events row (event-sourced attribute, per set-attribute-event-sourced.integration.test pattern) so it flows into the behavioral timeline; the attribute is readable on the profile.

SEND through the Dispatcher: take the outbox row the runner produced (automation_id set, dedupe_key = automation:<camp>:<profile>:<sendNodeId>) and run the dispatcher core (SES mocked via aws-sdk-client-mock, suppression→cap→quiet-hours→verified-domain gate) → assert a messages_log row with automation_id = the automation, ses_message_id set, status='sent', and SES SendEmail called with the workspace Configuration Set AFTER the gates (never a real send).

WEBHOOK fired via the INJECTED client (never a real host): assert exactly one captured request to the allowlisted URL, method/headers/rendered body correct ({{customer.*}} merged), the encrypted auth header decrypted at call time only; an SSRF/blocked target (loopback/169.254/non-allowlist) is refused and the failure is ISOLATED (recorded in activity_log source='webhook', tick does not crash, enrollment still advances).

IDEMPOTENCY: re-running the final tick (same enrollmentId) does NOT create a second outbox row (UNIQUE dedupe_key), does NOT re-send (messages_log stays 1), does NOT re-fire the webhook (per-node dedupe marker), and the enrollment stays 'completed'.

WORKSPACE-SCOPING: a parallel WS_B automation/enrollment with identical structure is untouched by the WS_A ticks; every write asserted scoped to the enrollment's workspace_id (runStatementsInWorkspaceTx guard); the dispatcher messages_log is in WS_A only.
testFiles.criterion: The full journey acceptance test passes: a built automation enrolls (segment/event/manual), advances through wait/hour-window/if/send/webhook/exit with an injected clock, sends via the Dispatcher (messages_log, automation_id) and fires the mocked webhook, idempotently, all workspace-scoped.

assertions: On #/automations/new build trigger→wait→hour_of_day_window→if→update-profile→send→webhook→exit via the (+) palette (reuse automation-builder spec helpers), configure each node via its drawer editor (reuse automation-node-editors patterns), attach a sendable template to the send node, configure the webhook node URL to a seeded-allowlisted host.
Save round-trips (toast); Publish flips status to 'active' on the list; the list row shows status 'active' + zero-initialized enrollment counts.
This is a thin browser smoke ONLY (no runner tick in-browser) — the advance/send/webhook assertions live in the integration test above.
testFiles.criterion: Full journey UI smoke: build the full-node journey in the browser and publish it (the runner advance is covered by the integration test).

assertions: Seed an active automation + an enrollment due now; pause the automation (status='paused'); runEnrollment(injected now) for that enrollment → result is a no-op/'skipped' (enrollment left at its node, status unchanged, no outbox/send) because the in-tx re-check requires the automation status='active'.
Resume (status='active') then runEnrollment again → it advances normally (proves pause is a reversible halt, not a data mutation on the enrollment).

If the current runner does NOT yet read automation.status inside the tick, this is the failing test that drives adding that gate in run.ts (load automation status alongside definition in the locked read; honor 'active' only); idempotency/concurrency guarantees preserved.
testFiles.criterion: Lifecycle pause halts advancement: a paused automation's due enrollment is not advanced by the runner.

Enrollment counts: a row shows {active/completed/exited/failed} reflecting automation_enrollments after an enroll.

The journey test composes the existing real-Postgres patterns into ONE end-to-end: enroll via the LIVE local-api POST /profiles/:id/events (closes the phase-3 gap — handlers.ts sendProfileEvent already calls enrollFromEvent on the same tx, so this is exercising the wired path, not new code) PLUS manual+segment enroll, then tick through wait→hour-window→if→set_attribute→send→webhook→exit, asserting: an event-sourced attribute write, an outbox→dispatcher messages_log row with automation_id (SES mocked, gated), the injected webhook fired once (SSRF/allowlist enforced, failures isolated), idempotent re-tick (dedupe_key + per-node webhook marker), all workspace-scoped (runStatementsInWorkspaceTx guard + a parallel WS_B left untouched). The ONLY backend behavior that may be genuinely missing is the runner reading automation.status to halt a PAUSED automation — the paused-automation-not-advanced test drives adding that gate inside the locked tick (preserving FOR UPDATE/CAS/idempotency); everything else (webhook, set-attribute, send, hour-window, all three enroll kinds) already has green integration coverage, so phase-7's real new surface is the UI list/detail split + lifecycle endpoints/counts + the combined acceptance test.
pureFunctions: automationCountsShape(rows): fold the GROUP BY (automation_id,status) rows into a per-automation {active,completed,exited,failed} record defaulting missing buckets to 0 (testable without DB; used to shape GET /automations).

nextLifecycle(current, action): map (status, 'pause'|'resume'|'archive') → next status or a typed rejection reason (e.g. resume only from 'paused'); pure transition table reused by the handler and a unit test.
isEnrollableAutomationStatus(status): boolean — only 'active' enrolls/advances (reused by the pause gate + enroll cores assertion).

REUSE existing pures: computeWaitNextRunAt, parseIso8601DurationSeconds, isWaitElapsed, processNode, evaluateBranch, buildBranchMatchQuery, parseEventEnrollmentTrigger, evaluateEventPayloadFilter, buildEnrollmentInsert, decideReenrollment, zonedInputToUtcIso/tzOffsetMs (hour-window + wait tz math) — no new tz code.
reuse: BroadcastComposer.tsx: the list+detail routing model, ActionMenu row actions, agoLabel/whenLabel, the no-domain banner pattern, status Badge+toneFor, spinner+lock send button wiring — MIRROR for AutomationsList.
AppShell.tsx broadcasts branch (rest === 'new' ? <Wizard/> : <Wizard id/>) — copy for automations list vs detail.

automation-runner: runEnrollment + RunDeps (injected now/sqs/webhookClient/withTx), buildSweepQuery, processNode, computeWaitNextRunAt, hour-window logic, set_attribute event-sourcing, the per-(automation,profile,node) dedupe_key — the journey test composes these EXACTLY as full-lifecycle.integration.test + webhook-node-tick + set-attribute-event-sourced + hour-window.integration already do.
automation-send-dispatcher-instance.integration.test.ts: the runner-outbox → dispatcher-core → messages_log(automation_id) chaining (SES mocked) — the journey test's send leg copies this.

enroll-from-event.integration.test.ts: the enrollFromEvent assertions; the journey test EXTENDS it by driving the LIVE local-api POST /profiles/:id/events (createApp over the real pool) instead of calling the core directly.

local-api handlers: scopedQuery, ownsResource, validateDefinitionOr400, enrollDepsOnClient/enrollDepsOnPool, the existing activateAutomation gate (publish) — the new pause/resume/archive sit beside activate and reuse scopedQuery + the 404 pattern.
ui kit: ActionMenu, Badge, Button (promise auto-lock), PageHeader, EmptyState, Card; ui/dialog askConfirm; ui/toast showToast — all already used by BroadcastComposer.
web/e2e/helpers.ts (loginAs, DEV_MKT/DEV_ACCT) + seed.ts (CAMP_A, DEV_* logins, sendable template) — reuse for the new e2e specs.
@cdp/shared timeZoneList/zonedInputToUtcIso/tzOffsetMs — the ONE tz implementation for hour-window + wait math (no new tz code).

assertions: loginAs(DEV_MKT) → nav-automations → the LIST screen renders (new testid automations-list-screen / list testid automation-list), NOT the canvas: page.getByTestId('automation-canvas') has count 0 and page.getByTestId('automation-builder') is NOT present on the list route (#/automations).
The list page has NO top-level Design email button: await expect(page.getByTestId('design-email')).toHaveCount(0) while on #/automations (regression for the removed PageHeader affordance).

The list has a 'New automation' action (automation-new); clicking it navigates to a SEPARATE detail route (assert page.url() matches /#\/automations\/(new|[0-9a-f-]{36})$/ and automation-canvas becomes visible) — the builder is NOT the top-level page.

Opening the seeded 'Welcome journey' row (automation-open / row click) navigates to #/automations/<CAMP_A> and the canvas reconstructs the DSL (node-trigger/node-wait/node-condition visible) — list→detail routing mirrors broadcasts.

From inside the detail builder, a send node's editor still exposes Design email (open node-send editor drawer → design-email present) — proving email design lives ONLY inside a send-node editor, not on the list.
Each list row shows the lifecycle status badge (automation-status) and an enrollment-counts summary cell (new testid automation-counts showing active/completed/exited/failed).
Back navigation from the detail builder returns to the list (a automations-back button mirroring broadcasts-back) → #/automations.
testFiles.criterion: The automations screen is a LIST (like broadcasts); creating/opening a automation routes to a SEPARATE /automations/:id builder page; there is NO "Design email" button on the automations list page (email design is only inside a send-node editor). Verified in a REAL browser.

assertions: Build a minimal valid trigger→send→exit automation in the detail page, attach a sendable seeded template copy to the send node (reuse the publish-gate spec's attach flow), Publish → status badge flips to 'active' on the list (automation-status text 'active').

An ActionMenu (automation-actions kebab, mirroring broadcast-actions) on each list row exposes: Open/Edit, Pause (when active), Resume (when paused), Archive — each a server-calling button that spins+locks (no native confirm; Archive uses askConfirm → app-dialog).

Pause an active automation → POST /automations/:id/pause → status badge becomes 'paused'; Resume → POST /automations/:id/resume → back to 'active'; Archive → POST /automations/:id/archive → status 'archived' and the row drops from the default list (or shows archived filter).

Capability gating: log in as DEV_ACCT (accounting, lacks manage_content) — the Pause/Resume/Publish/Archive actions are absent/disabled and a direct POST returns 403 (assert via role-visibility pattern); the marketer (manage_content) sees and can use them.
Workspace-scoping: the WS_A automation list never shows WS_B automations; switching workspace re-scopes the list (AppShell key remounts).

Enrollment counts render live: after enrolling a profile (via manual enroll in the builder or seeded enrollment) the row's automation-counts active count reflects automation_enrollments grouped by status for that automation only (workspace-scoped).
testFiles.criterion: The automation list shows lifecycle status + enrollment counts; publish/pause/resume work and are capability-gated + workspace-scoped; an active automation enrolls + advances profiles. Verified in a REAL browser.

assertions: POST /automations/:id/pause on an 'active' automation sets status='paused' (UPDATE ... SET status='paused' WHERE id AND workspace_id); returns the new status; idempotent (pausing a paused automation is a no-op 200, not an error).

POST /automations/:id/resume on a 'paused' automation sets status='active'; resume on a non-paused (draft/archived) automation is rejected 409 with a typed reason (can only resume a paused automation).

POST /automations/:id/archive sets status='archived' from any state; an archived automation is excluded from enrollment (enrollFromEvent/enrollFromSegmentChange only target status='active') — regression-assert no new enrollment after archive.

Pause does NOT touch in-flight automation_enrollments rows directly; assert the runner skips a paused automation's enrollments (the sweep/runEnrollment re-checks automation status='active' inside the lock; a paused automation's due enrollment is left parked, not advanced) — drives this with the automation-runner core in the journey test, asserted here at the handler level that status flips only.

Tenant isolation (inv.1/inv.2): a pause/resume/archive POST with a :id belonging to WS_B under a WS_A token returns 404 (scopedQuery WHERE workspace_id = ctx.workspaceId AND id = $1, rowCount 0 → 404); workspace_id is never taken from the body.
Capability: routes.ts maps POST /automations/:id/{pause,resume,archive} to manage_content; a marketer passes, an accounting role 403s (dispatch enforce).
testFiles.criterion: publish/pause/resume/archive work and are capability-gated + workspace-scoped (REAL Postgres).

assertions: GET /automations returns each automation with status AND a counts object {active, completed, exited, failed} aggregated from automation_enrollments grouped by status, scoped to ctx.workspaceId and automation_id (LEFT JOIN / lateral count so a automation with no enrollments returns all-zero counts).

Counts are per-automation and workspace-scoped: seed two automations in WS_A with differing enrollment status mixes + one automation in WS_B; assert WS_A list shows the correct per-automation split and never sums WS_B rows.

A single workspace-scoped aggregate query (one round-trip, GROUP BY automation_id, status) — assert correctness for active/completed/exited/failed buckets and that an unknown status (defensive) does not break the shape.
GET /automations/:id continues to return the full definition + timezone unchanged (regression).
testFiles.criterion: The automation list shows lifecycle status + enrollment counts (REAL Postgres).

assertions: Setup against REAL Postgres (adminPool): one workspace (settings.timezone set, e.g. 'America/New_York'), a profile with email, a verified sending_domain + domain_senders row, an email_templates copy (kind='copy') with subject+sender_id+to_address set (sendable), and a automation whose definition is trigger(event)→wait→hour_of_day_window→condition→action(set_attribute)→action(send)→action(webhook)→exit, status='active', with the webhook url on a per-workspace allowlist host.

LIVE EVENT ENROLLMENT (closes the phase-3 gap): drive enrollment through the local-api createApp() by POSTing to POST /profiles/:id/events (the live route, marketer token) with the trigger eventType+payload → assert exactly ONE automation_enrollments row at current_node=trigger, status='active', workspace-scoped; replaying the same POST (different event_id) does NOT duplicate (ON CONFLICT once).

ALSO assert the other two trigger kinds enroll via their cores in the same workspace: enrollProfileManually (POST /automations/:id/enroll body {profile_id}) and enrollFromSegmentChange — each idempotent, each one row.

ADVANCE with an injected clock: build RunDeps with now()=fixed Date (reuse withWorkspaceTx/runStatementsInWorkspaceTx + an injected webhookClient capturing requests + a CapturingSqs). Tick at T0 → parks at wait; tick at T0+delay but OUTSIDE the hour window → parks/defers at hour_of_day_window (next_run_at moved into the window, DST-correct in the workspace tz); tick at a clock INSIDE the window → condition evaluates, set_attribute writes, send enqueues to outbox, webhook fires, enrollment completes.

UPDATE-PROFILE is event-sourced: assert set_attribute wrote BOTH the profiles.attributes change AND an events row (event-sourced attribute, per set-attribute-event-sourced.integration.test pattern) so it flows into the behavioral timeline; the attribute is readable on the profile.

SEND through the Dispatcher: take the outbox row the runner produced (automation_id set, dedupe_key = automation:<camp>:<profile>:<sendNodeId>) and run the dispatcher core (SES mocked via aws-sdk-client-mock, suppression→cap→quiet-hours→verified-domain gate) → assert a messages_log row with automation_id = the automation, ses_message_id set, status='sent', and SES SendEmail called with the workspace Configuration Set AFTER the gates (never a real send).

WEBHOOK fired via the INJECTED client (never a real host): assert exactly one captured request to the allowlisted URL, method/headers/rendered body correct ({{customer.*}} merged), the encrypted auth header decrypted at call time only; an SSRF/blocked target (loopback/169.254/non-allowlist) is refused and the failure is ISOLATED (recorded in activity_log source='webhook', tick does not crash, enrollment still advances).

IDEMPOTENCY: re-running the final tick (same enrollmentId) does NOT create a second outbox row (UNIQUE dedupe_key), does NOT re-send (messages_log stays 1), does NOT re-fire the webhook (per-node dedupe marker), and the enrollment stays 'completed'.

WORKSPACE-SCOPING: a parallel WS_B automation/enrollment with identical structure is untouched by the WS_A ticks; every write asserted scoped to the enrollment's workspace_id (runStatementsInWorkspaceTx guard); the dispatcher messages_log is in WS_A only.
testFiles.criterion: The full journey acceptance test passes: a built automation enrolls (segment/event/manual), advances through wait/hour-window/if/send/webhook/exit with an injected clock, sends via the Dispatcher (messages_log, automation_id) and fires the mocked webhook, idempotently, all workspace-scoped.

assertions: On #/automations/new build trigger→wait→hour_of_day_window→if→update-profile→send→webhook→exit via the (+) palette (reuse automation-builder spec helpers), configure each node via its drawer editor (reuse automation-node-editors patterns), attach a sendable template to the send node, configure the webhook node URL to a seeded-allowlisted host.
Save round-trips (toast); Publish flips status to 'active' on the list; the list row shows status 'active' + zero-initialized enrollment counts.
This is a thin browser smoke ONLY (no runner tick in-browser) — the advance/send/webhook assertions live in the integration test above.
testFiles.criterion: Full journey UI smoke: build the full-node journey in the browser and publish it (the runner advance is covered by the integration test).

assertions: Seed an active automation + an enrollment due now; pause the automation (status='paused'); runEnrollment(injected now) for that enrollment → result is a no-op/'skipped' (enrollment left at its node, status unchanged, no outbox/send) because the in-tx re-check requires the automation status='active'.
Resume (status='active') then runEnrollment again → it advances normally (proves pause is a reversible halt, not a data mutation on the enrollment).

If the current runner does NOT yet read automation.status inside the tick, this is the failing test that drives adding that gate in run.ts (load automation status alongside definition in the locked read; honor 'active' only); idempotency/concurrency guarantees preserved.
testFiles.criterion: Lifecycle pause halts advancement: a paused automation's due enrollment is not advanced by the runner.
